column10: in_class_description_detail

Natural formations of the primary type, closed stand with
trees and shrubs reaching various heights between 5 and 50 m; the
tree crowns are relatively close together;
the overall canopy remains dense (coverage greater than
70%) Evergreen rainforest, semi-deciduous (mesophilic) forest, mountain forest, low mountain forest, coastal forest, dry forest
Natural formations, open stand in the Sudanese zone (north); Cover: 30% to 70%; Height: 8 to 15m

A forest whose canopy is continuous above a river or small stream, or a wetland (the presence of water may be temporary). Forest on hydromorphic soil, floodable/periodically flooded forest, gallery forest/riparian forest

Forest formation under the influence of degradation and regenerating forest regrowth, but whose cover is nevertheless
greater than 30%; structure consisting of successive stages of
unstable vegetation development. The difference is marked by
the structure/composition of species compared to neighboring primary
forests. Old secondary forest, young secondary forest, swamp/riparian secondary forest, mountain secondary forest

A monotonous formation bordering the maritime and lagoonal coastline, suitable for temporary immersion, in muddy beaches covered with ferns. Evergreen mangrove stands with a cover density exceeding 30%, featuring a herbaceous layer of coastal grasses or water bodies and upright clumps of ferns.
Woodland plots or habitat regeneration; tree height greater than 5m and canopy cover greater than 30%. Mixed forest plantations, agroforestry plantations

Forest formation (coverage greater than 30%) established on hydromorphic soils (valley, estuary, lowlands, marshes), near watercourses (river, reservoir, stream), where the presence of water is almost permanent. Swamp forest, forest on hydromorphic soil, floodplain forest/periodically flooded forest, etc.

Coffee plantation established with a low density (fewer than 20 trees per hectare) of forest trees at least 5 meters tall (full sun coffee). Coffee plantation established by association or preservation of more than 20 forest trees of at least 5 meters in height per hectare and constituting a shade rate greater than 40% (shade-grown coffee).Full sun coffee plantation, Shade coffee plantation

Cocoa trees (Theobroma cacao), with a dense canopy at maturity (50 to 100%), with a low density (fewer than 20 trees per hectare) of forest trees at least 5 meters tall (full sun cocoa).Cocoa trees (Theobroma cacao) with a dense canopy at maturity (50 to 100%) established through association or preservation with more than 20 forest trees at least 5 meters tall per hectare, providing a shade coverage greater than 40% (shade-grown cocoa).Full sun cocoa plantation, Shade-grown cocoa plantation
Rubber trees in full sun, rubber trees mixed with other trees.
Agricultural plot of oil palms, arranged in an equilateral triangle 9m per side; density 143 trees per hectare. Palm grove
Coconut palm grove in the coastal area, planted in rows spaced several meters apart; with a lifespan of one hundred years.

Cashew tree plantation, 5-15m tall, with a spreading crown and
dark green, evergreen foliage.
Cultivated in the Sudanian zone, the cashew tree
is planted in a 10m x 10m grid, in
full sun, in light, sandy,
deep, well-drained soils.

Areas of other undifferentiated cultivated land and various orchards, including lowland crops and young fallows between 3 and 7 years old (generally composed of post-cultivation grass regrowth).
These crops are short-cycle and long-cycle annuals (rice, yam, maize, cassava, pineapple plantations, sugarcane, cotton, etc.).

Note: These cultivated areas are identifiable plots on satellite images by their sometimes regular geometric shape.
Bare agricultural soil, rainfed rice cultivation, yam, maize, cassava, other cereals (millet, fonio, sorghum), pineapple plantations, sugarcane, cotton plantations, post-cultivation grass regrowth, agricultural marshland, rice paddies, etc.

Savanna on well-drained soil with a tree layer (5-20 m tall; 10-30% canopy cover); a shrub layer (2-5 m tall; less than 50% canopy cover); and a dense, continuous herbaceous layer at least 80 cm high.

Vegetation formation consisting of shrubs, less than 5m in height, scattered over a continuous herbaceous layer; and having:
- a shrub layer with at least 50% cover;
- a herbaceous layer with 10 to 100% cover;
Note: the tree layer is less than 10% cover.
This class also includes thickets, which are closed, dense formations with more than 80% cover, composed solely of shrubs less than 5m in height (a dense, young woodland).
Note: In these thickets, the grassy understory is absent or reduced to its simplest form.
Shrub savanna, fan palm savanna, swamp savanna, high-altitude meadow, coastal thicket, marshy thicket, riparian/floodplain thicket, secondary thicket

Natural grassy formations less than 2.5m high or short-grass growing on well-drained firm ground; woody vegetation is insignificant (no trees), consisting of only a few isolated shrubs, bushes, and subshrubs, or grouped in patches.
Grassy savanna, coastal savanna, high-altitude grassland, coastal grassland, grassy regrowth on bare soil (non-agricultural/straw stage of dys)

column9: Body of water, Courses and waterways
column10: A collection of depressions containing water in the form of a reservoir (body of water). A linear, interconnected hydrographic network flowing towards a body of water and a wide, interconnected watercourse (rivers and waterways).

Wet and muddy lands consisting of marshes and swamps,
where the soil is covered, permanently or intermittently, by a
shallow layer of stagnant water covered with vegetation.
Hydrophilous formations (helophytes and hydrophytes), of
Cyperaceae, develop there, either isolated or in the form of grassy mats.
Aquatic meadow, marshy meadow,
riparian/floodplain meadow

Areas resulting from human activities, other than agriculture, for the purpose of urbanization and various constructions (housing, industrial activities, mining operations, quarries, gold panning, land clearing, etc.)
Lines for the construction of communication infrastructure, transport routes and road, electrical, and rail networks
Urban housing, rural housing, encampments, industrial activity zones, mining operations, quarry zones, gold panning zones, cleared/deforested areas, burned areas
Road infrastructure, electrical infrastructure, rail infrastructure

Exposed rocks, granite domes often covered with
herbaceous vegetation, more or less discontinuous; less than 60 cm high
Rocky outcrops, vegetation on
rocky outcrops
Land devoid of natural vegetation cover (herbaceous, shrubby, arboreal), not constituting a cultivated area. Bare, non-agricultural soil, sandy areas.
